content.full_text:
March 14, 1978

Mr. Bradner Petersen
100 Universal City Plaza
Universal City, California 91608

Dear Mr. Petersen:

Thank you for your letter of January 21, 1978.

I refer you first to the language of Government Code Section 84204.1(a)(3):
Unless a major donor's primary political activity relates to the local
jurisdiction, he is qualified to use the abbreviated statement, Form 480,
in connection with elections occurring at any time other than the state
Direct Primary or General elections in even-numbered years.

The Form 480 is filed only in the local jurisdiction in which the major
donor's activity takes place. However, by 84204.1(b) the information
contained in the abbreviated statement must be repeated in any subsequent
statement filed, except that the itemization of contributions and expendi-
tures may be incorporated by reference if the subsequent statement and
copies are filed with the same officers as the abbreviated statement.
In addition, by Section 82013 an abbreviated statement does not qualify
as a post-election statement, and the amounts reported on abbreviated
statements must be cumulated forward to the next post-election statement,
or, in the case of a major donor, to the end of the calendar year if no
post-election statments are filed during the year.

When the next regular statement, i.e., a semi-annual statement (see
Government Code Section 84206) or a statement in connection with a
state Direct Primary or General election in even-numbered years is
filed, it will be necessary to pick up, repeat, and carry forward the
information reported on any abbreviated statements filed since the last
semi-annual or state election statement. This semi-annual statement,
or a statement filed in connection with the state Direct Primary or
General election in even-numbered years must be filed with the relevant
filing officers for each candidate supported during the cumulation
cycle covered (see Government Code Section 81005), including those
local jurisdictions in which abbreviated statements were filed during
that cumulation cycle.

Mr. Bradner Petersen                    -2-                    March 14, 1978


I realize this answer may do more to confuse than illuminate, but you
have asked a very complex question in general terms. If you have a
specific problem in which you need advice, please call me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,


John Giansello
Political Reform Analyst

JG:bw

BRADNER PETERSEN
ATTORNEY AT LAW

100 UNIVERSAL CITY PLAZA
UNIVERSAL CITY, CALIFORNIA 91608
(213) 985-4321

January 21, 1978


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 Kay Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen:

A major donor which from time to time files copies of campaign
statements with various governmental officials, desires a clari-
fication or interpretation of Part IV of your Information Manual
on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act.
Part IV is entitled "Where Must Statements Be Filed?"

If a campaign statement is filed with the City Clerk of a city
with reference to contributions to candidates for or persons
holding elective office in the city involved, is it also necessary
to report to the City Clerk contributions during the same filing
period to candidates for statewide office? Similarly if campaign
statements are filed with the Secretary of State (plus the Regis-
trar-Recorder of Los Angeles County and the Registrar of Voters
of San Francisco) with reference to contributions to candidates
for or persons holding statewide elective office, is it also
necessary to report to the Secretary of State, etc. contributions
during the same filing period to candidates for office in a city?

I prefer that this be treated as a request for an informal clari-
fication or interpretation. If that is not possible, would you
kindly process this as a request for an opinion.

Very truly yours,

Bradner Petersen
Bradner Petersen


BP:eh

sections.question: If a campaign statement is filed with the City Clerk of a city with reference to contributions to candidates for or persons holding elective office in the city involved, is it also necessary to report to the City Clerk contributions during the same filing period to candidates for statewide office? Similarly if campaign statements are filed with the Secretary of State (plus the Registrar-Recorder of Los Angeles County and the Registrar of Voters of San Francisco) with reference to contributions to candidates for or persons holding statewide elective office, is it also necessary to report to the Secretary of State, etc. contributions during the same filing period to candidates for office in a city?
sections.conclusion: by Section 82018 an abbreviated statement does not qualify unless filed on abbreviated statement forms. On the next post-election statement, it is not treated as a post-election statement, and amounts will be cumulated to the end of the applicable calendar year. If no other statements are filed during the year, the information will be carried forward to the next regular statement, i.e., a semi-annual statement or a statement in connection with a state Direct Primary or General election in even-numbered years. When filing the next regular statement, it will be necessary to pick up, repeat, and carry forward the information reported on any abbreviated statements filed since the last local or state election statement, and this statement must comply with the relevant reporting requirements for each jurisdiction during the cumulation cycle.
sections.question_synthetic: A major donor seeks clarification on whether campaign statements filed with local city clerks regarding local candidates must also include contributions to statewide candidates in the same filing period, and conversely, whether statements filed with the Secretary of State regarding statewide candidates must also include contributions to local candidates.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Campaign statements must be filed separately with each jurisdiction (local and state) for contributions relevant to candidates in that jurisdiction; abbreviated statements filed locally do not satisfy state filing requirements, and amounts from abbreviated statements must be carried forward and cumulated in the next regular statement.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document quality is poor with significant OCR errors and fragmentation. The question section is clearly from the incoming request (dated January 21, 1978 from Bradner Petersen). The conclusion is FPPC's response, partially legible. The response signature is from John Giansello, Political Analyst. High confidence in document type and question extraction, moderate confidence in conclusion due to OCR degradation.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: If a campaign statement is filed with the City Clerk of a city with reference to contributions to candidates for or persons holding elective office in the city involved, is it also necessary to report to the City Clerk contributions during the same filing period to candidates for statewide office? Similarly if campaign statements are filed with the Secretary of State (plus the Registrar-Recorder of Los Angeles County and the Registrar of Voters of San Francisco) with reference to contributions to candidates for or persons holding statewide elective office, is it also necessary to report to the Secretary of State, etc. contributions during the same filing period to candidates for office in a city?

CONCLUSION: by Section 82018 an abbreviated statement does not qualify unless filed on abbreviated statement forms. On the next post-election statement, it is not treated as a post-election statement, and amounts will be cumulated to the end of the applicable calendar year. If no other statements are filed during the year, the information will be carried forward to the next regular statement, i.e., a semi-annual statement or a statement in connection with a state Direct Primary or General election in even-numbered years. When filing the next regular statement, it will be necessary to pick up, repeat, and carry forward the information reported on any abbreviated statements filed since the last local or state election statement, and this statement must comply with the relevant reporting requirements for each jurisdiction during the cumulation cycle.

embedding.summary: An attorney representing a major donor requested clarification on whether campaign disclosure statements filed with local city clerks regarding local candidates must also include statewide candidate contributions, and vice versa. The FPPC advised that separate filings are required for each jurisdiction and explained the requirements for cumulating abbreviated statements in subsequent regular statements.
